schema_json.description:
LLM contract for the agentic search loop 'Reason' step.

After each round of searches, the LLM analyzes results and decides
whether to continue searching or converge. Generates targeted
follow-up queries informed by what was found so far.

key_findings.description: 3-5 key findings from the latest round
key_findings.title: Key Findings

perspective_gaps.description: Underrepresented STORM perspectives in current results
perspective_gaps.title: Perspective Gaps

web_queries.description: 3-6 web queries for the next round
web_queries.title: Web Queries

academic_queries.description: 1-3 Semantic Scholar queries using precise terminology
academic_queries.title: Academic Queries

exa_queries.description: 0-1 semantic queries for Exa neural search
exa_queries.title: Exa Queries

should_continue.description: Whether another search round is needed
should_continue.title: Should Continue

reasoning.description: Brief rationale for the convergence assessment
reasoning.title: Reasoning

knowledge_gaps.description: Specific knowledge gaps identified from reading content
knowledge_gaps.title: Knowledge Gaps

schema_json.title: AgenticRoundAnalysis

prompt:
You are a research search strategist conducting an agentic deep research loop.

RESEARCH QUESTION: What are the proven health benefits and risks of intermittent fasting?

ROUND HISTORY:
Round 1: 9 queries -> 80 web + 34 academic, 114 new URLs, 2 pages read

KNOWLEDGE ACQUIRED (2 pages read so far):
[Page 1] Intermittent fasting improves metabolic outcomes in adults with metabolic syndro
  Summary: This 2025 systematic review and meta-analysis evaluated the effects of intermittent fasting (IF) on glycemic control, lipid profile, and inflammatory markers in adults with metabolic syndrome. The authors searched PubMed, Embase, Cochrane, Scopus, and Web of Science through September 2025, registered the protocol with PROSPERO (CRD420251142741), and used Cochrane RoB2 and GRADE to assess evidence quality. Ten randomized controlled trials encompassing 701 participants met inclusion criteria (adul
  Perspectives: Scientific, Methodological, Public_Health
  Key facts: 10 RCTs with 701 adults with metabolic syndrome included; searches through September 2025; Fasting blood sugar reduction: SMD -0.51 (95% CI -0.81 to -0.20, p=0.001); LDL-C reduction: SMD -0.34 (95% CI -0.53 to -0.14, p=0.001); IL-6 reduction: SMD -0.30 (p=0.029)
[Page 2] Effects of Intermittent Fasting on Regulation of Metabolic Homeostasis: A System
  Summary: This systematic review and meta-analysis compared Alternate-Day Fasting (ADF), Time-Restricted Fasting (TRF), and Religious Fasting (RF) across obesity, type 2 diabetes (T2D), and metabolic syndrome (MetS) populations. Searches covered PubMed, Scopus, Trip Database, Web of Knowledge, and Embase through June 2022, yielding 64 qualifying reports for qualitative analysis and 47 for quantitative synthesis (n=4,052 participants; 2,007 men, 1,977 women, 68 undisclosed). The review stratifies four IF s
  Perspectives: Scientific, Methodological, Public_Health
  Key facts: 64 reports qualified for qualitative analysis; 47 entered quantitative meta-analysis; search cutoff June 2022; 4,052 total participants (2,007 men, 1,977 women, 68 undisclosed) across studies; 19 studies targeted T2D, 35 targeted obese/overweight, 7 MetS, 6 healthy controls

NEW SEARCH RESULTS (round 1, titles only — not yet read):

- Cost-Effectiveness of Varying Weight Loss Interventions - AJMC
- [PDF] Semaglutide and Tirzepatide for Obesity: Effectiveness and Value
- A health economic model to assess the cost-effectiveness ... - PubMed
- Weight Loss Treatment Variability and Cost-Effectiveness - AJMC
- Cost Effectiveness of Two Lifestyle Interventions in the Vermont ...
- [PDF] Overweight and obesity management: Diet interventions - NICE
- Cost-Effectiveness and Long-Term Savings of the Bright Bodies ...
- Cost Effective Interventions That Can Prevent Obesity & Chronic ...
- Intermittent Fasting: Efficacy, Safety, and Its Impact on Body Weight ...
- A National Study Exploring the Association between Fasting ... - PMC
- 8-hour time-restricted eating linked to a 91% higher risk of ...
- The Effects of Intermittent Fasting on Cardiovascular Health of ...
- Intermittent fasting and changes in clinical risk scores - PMC
- A perspective on intermittent fasting and cardiovascular risk in the ...
- Intermittent Fasting Study Sparks Debate Over Increases in CV ...
- Roundup: New Study Intensifies Debate Over Intermittent Fasting ...
- Comparative effects of intermittent fasting and calorie restriction on ...
- Intermittent fasting: benefits or risks? Study raises questions ... - BBC
- Study suggests intermittent fasting nearly doubles risk of death from ...

PERSPECTIVE COVERAGE:
  Covered (7/9): Scientific, Regulatory, Industry, Economic, Public_Health, Methodological, Emerging_Trends
  Uncovered: Historical, Regional
  Low coverage: Regulatory, Industry, Economic

Based on what has been READ:
1. Decompose the research question into 5-8 sub-questions.
   Which sub-questions have ZERO or WEAK evidence?
   Which sub-questions are well-answered with strong evidence?
2. What CONTRADICTIONS exist between sources? Flag conflicting evidence.
3. What specific KNOWLEDGE GAPS remain? List them as specific factual questions
   we CANNOT yet answer. 'none' is almost never correct for a complex research question.
4. Rate overall confidence in accumulated knowledge: LOW/MEDIUM/HIGH with reasoning.
5. Which perspectives need more coverage?
6. Generate 3-6 targeted WEB queries to fill those gaps.
7. Generate 1-3 ACADEMIC queries using precise terminology.
8. Optionally generate 0-1 semantic EXA queries.
9. Assess ANSWER COMPLETENESS for the research question:
   - List specific sub-questions that remain UNANSWERED.
   - For each answered sub-question, rate evidence strength (weak/moderate/strong).
   - 'expanding': Major sub-questions still unanswered.
   - 'narrowing': Most sub-questions answered, seeking confirmation/depth.
   - 'saturated': All sub-questions answered with strong evidence.
10. Should we continue searching?

Focus on queries that address content-level gaps, not just missing URLs.
